Package com.rays.common

Class BaseDTO

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.rays.common.BaseDTO
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    DropdownList
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AttachmentDTO, CollegeDTO, CourseDTO, FacultyDTO, MarksheetDTO, RoleDTO, StudentDTO, SubjectDTO, TimeTableDTO, UserDTO
    @MappedSuperclass
public abstract class BaseDTO
extends java.lang.Object
implements DropdownList
    Abstract base class for all Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) in the application.

    Provides common persistence fields including primary key, audit columns (created/modified by and datetime), and declares abstract methods that each concrete DTO must implement for unique-key validation and labeling. Also implements the DropdownList interface to support use in dropdown controls.

    Author:
    Ajay Pratap Kerketta

      Modifier and Type Field Description

      The login ID of the user who created this record.

      The timestamp when this record was first created.

      The auto-generated primary key for the entity.
      protected java.lang.String modifiedBy
      The login ID of the user who last modified this record.
      protected java.sql.Timestamp modifiedDatetime
      The timestamp when this record was last modified.

      Returns the string representation of the primary key, as required by the DropdownList interface.
      abstract java.lang.String getLabel()
      Returns a human-readable label for the entity, used in messages such as "College already exists".

      Returns the login ID of the user who last modified this record.

      Returns the timestamp when this record was last modified.

      Returns the name of the database table that this entity maps to.

      Returns the field name used as the unique key for duplicate-check validation.
      abstract java.lang.String getUniqueValue()
      Returns the value of the unique key field for duplicate-check validation.

    • Field Detail

      • id

        protected java.lang.Long id
        The auto-generated primary key for the entity. Uses a native generation strategy via Hibernate's GenericGenerator.

      • createdBy

        protected java.lang.String createdBy
        The login ID of the user who created this record.

      • modifiedBy

        protected java.lang.String modifiedBy
        The login ID of the user who last modified this record.

      • createdDatetime

        protected java.sql.Timestamp createdDatetime
        The timestamp when this record was first created.

      • modifiedDatetime

        protected java.sql.Timestamp modifiedDatetime
        The timestamp when this record was last modified.

    • Method Detail

      • getId

        public java.lang.Long getId()
        Returns the primary key of the entity.
        Returns:
        the Long ID, or null if not yet persisted

        public void setId​(java.lang.Long id)
        Sets the primary key of the entity.
        Parameters:
        id - the Long primary key value to set

      • getCreatedBy

        public java.lang.String getCreatedBy()
        Returns the login ID of the user who created this record.
        Returns:
        the creator's login ID as a String

      • setCreatedBy

        public void setCreatedBy​(java.lang.String createdBy)
        Sets the login ID of the user who created this record.
        Parameters:
        createdBy - the creator's login ID

      • getModifiedBy

        public java.lang.String getModifiedBy()
        Returns the login ID of the user who last modified this record.
        Returns:
        the modifier's login ID as a String

      • setModifiedBy

        public void setModifiedBy​(java.lang.String modifiedBy)
        Sets the login ID of the user who last modified this record.
        Parameters:
        modifiedBy - the modifier's login ID

      • getCreatedDatetime

        public java.sql.Timestamp getCreatedDatetime()
        Returns the timestamp when this record was created.
        Returns:
        the creation Timestamp

      • setCreatedDatetime

        public void setCreatedDatetime​(java.sql.Timestamp createdDatetime)
        Sets the timestamp when this record was created.
        Parameters:
        createdDatetime - the creation Timestamp to set

      • getModifiedDatetime

        public java.sql.Timestamp getModifiedDatetime()
        Returns the timestamp when this record was last modified.
        Returns:
        the last modification Timestamp

      • setModifiedDatetime

        public void setModifiedDatetime​(java.sql.Timestamp modifiedDatetime)
        Sets the timestamp when this record was last modified.
        Parameters:
        modifiedDatetime - the modification Timestamp to set

      • getUniqueKey

        public abstract java.lang.String getUniqueKey()
        Returns the field name used as the unique key for duplicate-check validation.

        For example, a CollegeDTO might return "name" to prevent duplicate college names.

        Returns:
        the entity attribute name used as the unique key, or null if no unique check is needed

      • getUniqueValue

        public abstract java.lang.String getUniqueValue()
        Returns the value of the unique key field for duplicate-check validation.
        Returns:
        the string value of the unique field to check against existing records

      • getLabel

        public abstract java.lang.String getLabel()
        Returns a human-readable label for the entity, used in messages such as "College already exists".
        Returns:
        the display label for this entity type as a String

      • getTableName

        public abstract java.lang.String getTableName()
        Returns the name of the database table that this entity maps to.
        Returns:
        the table name as a String

      • getKey

        public java.lang.String getKey()
        Returns the string representation of the primary key, as required by the DropdownList interface.
        Specified by:
        getKey in interface DropdownList
        Returns:
        the primary key as a String
